walkthrough.title: Assemble the Dispenser
walkthrough.text: Welcome to the Horribilorum Sorcery Academy! Your final exam begins now. First, take the plastic Dispenser and set it in the center of the table where all players can reach it. This magnificent contraption has five slide tracks where ingredient marbles will roll down, plus a tank at the top to hold the reserve marbles. Think of it as your magical ingredient vending machine.

walkthrough.title: Hand Out Desk Boards
walkthrough.text: Each aspiring witch or wizard receives one Desk board. Place it in front of you with some space above and below it. Your Desk has two Bunsen burners on top where you will brew potions, and a small retort flask that serves as your Ingredient Pool. The Pool can hold up to 3 leftover ingredient marbles between turns.

walkthrough.title: Choose the First Player
walkthrough.text: The player who most recently prepared a drink in real life becomes the First Player and takes the First Player token. This token is just a reminder and never changes hands during the game. Play will proceed clockwise from the First Player.

walkthrough.title: Select Potion Types for This Game
walkthrough.text: There are 8 different kinds of Potions in the game, with 64 tiles total. Remove 2 of the 8 kinds from the game, either randomly or by choice, and put them back in the box. For your first game, the rulebook recommends leaving out the Balm of Uttermost Stickiness and the Filter of Lavamancing, as they are trickier to use. You will play with the remaining 6 kinds.

walkthrough.title: Draft Starter Potions
walkthrough.text: From the 6 remaining potion types, take 2 random starter potion tiles per player (marked with a special starter symbol) and lay them out in the middle of the table, Recipe side up. Starting from the First Player going clockwise, each player picks one starter potion. Then, starting with the last player going counterclockwise, each player picks a second. Place both starter potions on the two Bunsen burners of your Desk, Recipe side up.

walkthrough.title: Create the Potion Stacks
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all remaining potion tiles together and divide them into 5 roughly equal face-up stacks, with the Recipe side showing. Place these stacks near the Dispenser where everyone can see and reach them. When you complete a potion during the game, you will draw a replacement from the top of any of these stacks.

walkthrough.title: Fill the Dispenser with Marbles
walkthrough.text: Pour all 80 ingredient marbles into the tank at the top of the Dispenser. There are 20 marbles of each of the 4 colors: Unicorn Tears (blue), Dragon Smoke (red), Ogre Mucus (yellow), and Fairy Dandruff (black). Make sure the marbles roll down and completely fill all 5 slide tracks. If any tracks are not full, gently nudge marbles toward open tracks.

walkthrough.title: Set Out Tokens and Countdown Stack
walkthrough.text: Place all the Little Help tokens and remaining Skill tokens near the Dispenser. Then create the Skill token countdown stack: use 4 Skill tokens for 2 players, 5 for 3 players, or 6 for 4 players. When this countdown stack is depleted, the end of the game is triggered. Each Skill token is worth 4 points and each Little Help token costs you 2 points. You are now ready to brew!

walkthrough.title: Turn Overview
walkthrough.text: Potion Explosion is played in turns, clockwise around the table. On your turn, you MUST take 1 ingredient marble from the Dispenser as your regular pick. You MAY also drink completed potions and/or ask for a Little Help from the Professor. The magic happens when removing a marble causes others to collide and explode! Let's break down each part of your turn.

walkthrough.title: Taking Your Regular Pick
walkthrough.text: To begin your turn, choose any marble visible on the Dispenser's slide tracks and take it. You can pick from any position on any track, though you cannot take the 9th marble (the one barely peeking out at the very top of each track). After you remove a marble, all the marbles above it in that track will roll down to fill the gap. This sliding is what can trigger magical explosions!

walkthrough.title: Triggering Explosions
walkthrough.text: When marbles slide down after your pick, if two or more marbles of the same color collide, they EXPLODE! You collect all connected marbles of that explosion color. Here is the truly magical part: after removing those exploding marbles, more marbles slide down, and if another same-color collision occurs, that triggers yet another explosion! These chain reactions can cascade multiple times, netting you a huge haul of ingredients in a single turn.

walkthrough.title: Chain Reactions and the Middle Pick Trick
walkthrough.text: A clever trick many new students miss: if three or more marbles of the same color are in a row, you can pick one from the MIDDLE. The remaining marbles of that color collide with each other and still trigger an explosion! Also remember that an explosion only happens when two or more marbles of the same color collide with each other. Two different-colored marbles meeting does not cause an explosion.

walkthrough.title: A Little Help from the Professor
walkthrough.text: Once per turn (before or after your regular pick), you may ask for a Little Help. Take a Little Help token and then pick 1 additional marble of your choice from the Dispenser. This bonus pick NEVER triggers explosions, even if it causes same-color marbles to collide. However, each Little Help token costs you 2 points at the end of the game, so use this power sparingly! A wise student takes the Little Help before their regular pick to set up a bigger chain reaction.

walkthrough.title: Placing Ingredients on Potions
walkthrough.text: After collecting marbles, you must place them on matching colored holes on potions brewing on your Desk. A blue marble goes on a blue hole, red on red, and so on. Once placed, a marble cannot be moved until that potion is completed. If you have marbles that do not match any open holes, you may store up to 3 in your Ingredient Pool. Any marbles still in hand at the end of your turn that exceed your Pool's capacity must be returned to the Dispenser.

walkthrough.title: Completing a Potion
walkthrough.text: When all holes on a potion tile are filled with the correct colored marbles, that potion is complete! Return all the marbles from that tile to the Dispenser's tank, then flip the tile to its Completed side and move it to the area below your Desk. Completed potions are worth victory points AND can be drunk for a one-time magical power. You can complete at most 2 potions per turn (since you only have 2 Bunsen burners).

walkthrough.title: Drinking Potions
walkthrough.text: At any point during your turn, you may drink one or more of your completed potions to activate their magical effects. Each potion can only be drunk once. Rotate the tile upside-down to show it has been used. When a potion lets you take marbles from the Dispenser, those picks NEVER trigger explosions. Critically, a drunk potion still counts for points and for Skill token achievements, so drink away without fear!

walkthrough.title: End of Turn Cleanup
walkthrough.text: After you have taken your regular pick, optionally used a Little Help, and drunk any potions you wish, your turn ends. First, discard any marbles still in hand if your Pool is already full (up to 3). Then, if you have fewer than 2 potions brewing, draw new potion tiles from any of the 5 stacks and place them on your empty Bunsen burners. You may NOT move Pool ingredients onto newly drawn potions until your next turn.

walkthrough.title: Earning Skill Tokens
walkthrough.text: After cleanup, check if you earned any Skill tokens. You earn one Skill token when you complete your 3rd potion of the same kind (same power icon). You also earn one when you complete potions of 5 different kinds. Each achievement can only be earned once. A single potion can count toward both achievements. Each Skill token is worth 4 points at the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Game End Trigger
walkthrough.text: The end of the game is triggered when the last Skill token from the countdown stack is taken, or when there are no more potion tiles available. Once triggered, play continues until the player to the right of the First Player finishes their turn, so everyone plays the same number of turns. Players can still earn Skill tokens from the general pool after the countdown stack is empty.

walkthrough.title: Final Scoring
walkthrough.text: Add up the point values printed on all your completed potion tiles, plus 4 points for each Skill token you earned, then subtract 2 points for each Little Help token you collected. The player with the most points is the Student of the Year! In case of a tie, each tied player takes one marble from the Dispenser trying to trigger an explosion. The player who collects the most marbles from this tiebreaker wins.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Potions Lab!
walkthrough.text: Let's brew some potions together! We recommend playing your first game with 2 or 3 players so the Dispenser refills more predictably and you can focus on learning the marble-drafting mechanics. For this tutorial, we will walk through the first several turns of a 2-player game between you and a friend. Grab your Desk boards, set up the Dispenser, and let's begin your final exam!

walkthrough.title: Survey the Dispenser
walkthrough.text: Before grabbing a marble, take a moment to scan all 5 slide tracks. Look for clusters of same-colored marbles that are separated by just one differently-colored marble. Those are your explosion opportunities! If you remove that one different marble, the same-colored marbles above and below will collide and explode into your hands. Train your eyes to spot these setups.

walkthrough.title: Your First Pick
walkthrough.text: Look at your two starter potions on your Desk. What colors do you need? Now scan the Dispenser for a marble of that color that will also cause an explosion. Found one? Go ahead and take it! Watch the marbles slide down and see if same-colored marbles collide. If they do, scoop up all connected marbles of that color. Congratulations on your first explosion!

walkthrough.title: Place Your Ingredients
walkthrough.text: Now place the marbles you collected onto matching holes on your potions. Blue marble on a blue hole, red on red. If you have extras that do not match any open holes, tuck up to 3 into your Pool. Remember, your Pool is precious limited storage, so try not to fill it with colors you will not need soon.

walkthrough.title: Your Opponent Takes a Turn
walkthrough.text: Watch your opponent take their turn. Notice how they scan the Dispenser before picking. Pay attention to how the marble arrangement changes after their pick and explosions. The Dispenser is a shared resource, and what your opponent takes reshuffles the landscape for your next turn. The arrangement you see now will be different when it comes back to you!

walkthrough.title: Spotting a Chain Reaction
walkthrough.text: It is your turn again. This time, look for a chain reaction opportunity: a pick that triggers an explosion, and after removing those marbles, causes a SECOND collision of a different color. These double (or triple!) explosions are the heart of the game and can net you 5 or more marbles in a single pick. Even if the chain does not perfectly match your potion needs, the sheer volume of ingredients is worth it.

walkthrough.title: MISTAKE -- Missing an Explosion!
walkthrough.text: Oops! Did you just take a marble and not notice that two yellow marbles collided on the slide after your pick? This is the most common beginner mistake. After every single pick, watch the marbles settle and carefully check each track for same-color collisions. It is easy to get excited about the marble you grabbed and forget to claim the bonus marbles from the explosion. Always watch the slide!

walkthrough.title: Completing Your First Potion
walkthrough.text: By now, one of your potions should be close to complete. Focus your next pick on the color you need to fill the last hole. When the final marble clicks into place, all the marbles come off the tile and go back into the Dispenser's tank. Flip the tile to its Completed side and slide it below your Desk. You just brewed your first potion! That felt magical, didn't it?

walkthrough.title: Draw a New Potion
walkthrough.text: At the end of your turn, since you completed a potion, you now have an empty Bunsen burner. You must draw a new potion tile from the top of any of the 5 stacks. Look at what is available and consider which potions need colors that are abundant in the Dispenser right now. A potion that needs mostly blue when there are lots of blue marbles visible is a great pick!

walkthrough.title: Using the Little Help
walkthrough.text: It is a few turns in and you need just one more red marble to complete a potion, but there are no good red picks that trigger explosions. This is a perfect time to ask for a Little Help! Take a Little Help token and grab that red marble you need. Remember, this costs 2 points at end of game but completing the potion could be worth 4-7 points plus a usable power. Sometimes the math is clearly in your favor.

walkthrough.title: Strategic Little Help Timing
walkthrough.text: Here is a pro move: use the Little Help BEFORE your regular pick. If removing a marble with the Little Help creates a setup where same-colored marbles are now separated by just one marble, you can use your regular pick to remove that separator and trigger a massive chain reaction! The Little Help itself never triggers explosions, but it can sculpt the Dispenser for your real pick.

walkthrough.title: Drinking Your First Potion
walkthrough.text: You have a completed potion sitting below your Desk. Let's drink it! Look at the power on the back of the tile. For example, the Potion of Wisdom lets you take 1 marble of your choice from the Dispenser without triggering explosions. Rotate the tile upside-down to show it has been used. The potion still counts for its point value and toward Skill token achievements. Cheers!

walkthrough.title: Combining Potion Powers with Your Pick
walkthrough.text: The most satisfying turns come from combining a potion power with your regular pick. Drink a Potion of Magnetical Attraction to grab 2 adjacent marbles, use a Little Help to grab another, then make your regular pick to trigger a chain explosion. You could collect 8 or more marbles in a single turn! Mastering this combo approach is what separates casual brewers from true potion masters.

walkthrough.title: MISTAKE -- Hoarding Instead of Completing
walkthrough.text: A common trap for new players: stockpiling marbles in your Pool instead of focusing on completing potions. Your Pool only holds 3 marbles, and any extras at end of turn are discarded back to the Dispenser. Completed potions give you points AND powers you can use immediately. It is almost always better to finish a smaller potion quickly than to slowly accumulate marbles for a big one. Speed wins this exam!

walkthrough.title: Working Toward Skill Tokens
walkthrough.text: Check your completed potions. Are you close to completing 3 of the same kind, or 5 different kinds? Skill tokens are worth 4 points each, which is as much as a small potion! When choosing new potions to brew, keep an eye on what Skill token achievements you are closest to. Sometimes picking a potion of a kind you already have two of is better than a higher-point potion of a new kind.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Dispenser Like a Crystal Ball
walkthrough.text: By mid-game, you should start thinking one turn ahead. After your pick, look at how the marbles will settle. Will your opponent have juicy chain reactions available? Sometimes it is worth taking a slightly less optimal pick for yourself if it denies your opponent a massive chain. In a 2-player game, this defensive awareness matters even more since there is only one opponent to watch.

walkthrough.title: MISTAKE -- Ignoring Potion Powers
walkthrough.text: Some new players complete potions and just forget about the powers, treating them as pure point tiles. This is leaving magic on the table! Potion powers like Abyssal Draft (take 1 marble of each color from the bottom row) or Sands of Time (reactivate a used potion) can swing entire turns. Always check your completed potions before your pick. The best turn order is: consider drinking a potion, then take your Little Help if useful, then make your regular pick.

walkthrough.title: The Refilling Rule
walkthrough.text: When marbles go back into the Dispenser (from completed potions or discarded extras), you must roll them randomly onto the lid. Do not aim them into specific tracks! If a marble lands on a full track and sticks out, move it to the nearest track with space. This randomness keeps the game fresh and unpredictable. The Dispenser state is always evolving.

walkthrough.title: Approaching the Endgame
walkthrough.text: Keep an eye on the Skill token countdown stack. When it is getting low (1-2 tokens left), the game could end any turn. Shift your focus to completing whatever potions you can for maximum points. If you have a nearly-finished high-value potion and a barely-started low-value one, pour all your energy into the one that is almost done. Unfinished potions score zero!

walkthrough.title: The Final Scoring Tally
walkthrough.text: The game has ended! Let's count up. Add the point values on all your completed potion tiles. Add 4 points for each Skill token. Subtract 2 points for each Little Help token. A typical winning score in a 2-player game might be around 50-65 points. How did you do? Did those chain explosions pay off?

walkthrough.title: Reflecting on Your First Brew
walkthrough.text: Think back on this game. Did you spot chain reactions early? Did you remember to drink your potions for their powers? Did you focus on completing potions quickly rather than hoarding marbles? Each game of Potion Explosion teaches you to read the Dispenser better, combo your potions more cleverly, and time your Little Help tokens more shrewdly. Every game, you will see bigger and better chain reactions.

walkthrough.title: Adding the Advanced Potions
walkthrough.text: Now that you have mastered the basics, try adding the Balm of Uttermost Stickiness and the Filter of Lavamancing back into the game for your next session. These two potions have more situational but powerful effects that open up new tactical possibilities. You are ready to handle them now that you understand the flow of explosions and potion combos.

walkthrough.title: Go Brew Something Spectacular!
walkthrough.text: You have graduated from the tutorial! You know how to spot chain reactions, combo potions with your regular pick, manage your Pool, and race toward Skill tokens. The Horribilorum Sorcery Academy awaits your next exam. Gather your friends, fill that Dispenser, and go brew something spectacular!

summary.title: Go Brew Something Spectacular!
bullets: You know chain reactions, combos, Pool management, and Skill tokens
Time to brew on your own!

walkthrough.title: Always Scan Before You Grab
walkthrough.text: Resist the urge to grab the first marble that matches your potion. Before every pick, scan ALL five tracks top to bottom. Look for same-color pairs separated by a single marble. That single marble in between is your golden pick because removing it creates a collision. Spending 10 extra seconds scanning can turn a 1-marble turn into a 5-marble bonanza.

walkthrough.title: Complete Potions Quickly
walkthrough.text: Speed is everything in Potion Explosion. A completed potion gives you points, a usable power, and frees up a Bunsen burner for a new potion. Sitting on two half-finished potions for several turns means you are missing out on powers that could accelerate your game. When choosing new potions, grab ones that overlap with the colors already abundant in the Dispenser or in your Pool.

walkthrough.title: Use Potions Before Your Pick
walkthrough.text: The ideal turn order is: consider drinking a potion first, then use your Little Help if needed, then take your regular pick last. Potion powers and Little Help can rearrange the Dispenser or give you marbles that let you complete potions before you even make your main move. Your regular pick is the only action that triggers explosions, so save it for when the Dispenser is perfectly primed.

walkthrough.title: Manage Your Pool Wisely
walkthrough.text: Your 3-marble Pool is a precious resource. Do not fill it with random colors just because you can. Keep marbles that match your current potions or the potion you plan to draw next. If your Pool is full of useless colors, consider using them before end of turn by swapping them onto potions where possible, or accept the loss and let them go back to the Dispenser.

walkthrough.title: Little Help Is an Investment, Not a Failure
walkthrough.text: New players often avoid the Little Help because losing 2 points feels bad. But completing a potion worth 5 points by spending 1 Little Help is a net gain of 3 points PLUS you get the potion power. The math almost always favors taking the Little Help when it directly completes a potion. Think of it as a strategic investment, not a penalty.

walkthrough.title: Race Toward Skill Tokens
walkthrough.text: Skill tokens are worth 4 points each and they also bring the game closer to ending, which can catch unprepared opponents off guard. If you are one potion away from a Skill token (3rd of a kind or 5th different kind), prioritize that completion. The 4 bonus points plus the tempo advantage of ending the game on your terms is extremely powerful.

walkthrough.title: Watch Your Opponents
walkthrough.text: Glance at your opponents' Desks between turns. What colors do they need? If two players both need a lot of blue, blue marbles will drain fast. In tight games, you might even deny an opponent a key marble by taking it yourself, especially if it also triggers a useful explosion for you. Awareness of the whole table gives you an edge.

walkthrough.title: Embrace the Chaos
walkthrough.text: The Dispenser is inherently random, and sometimes the perfect chain reaction just is not there. Do not get frustrated. Take the best available pick, use your potions and Little Help to maximize value, and trust that the marbles will align in your favor soon. Flexibility and adaptability beat rigid planning every time in Potion Explosion.

bullets: Check all 5 tracks before every pick
Look for same-color pairs separated by 1 marble

summary.title: Complete Potions Quickly
bullets: Speed = more points, more powers, more Bunsen burner turnover
Pick potions matching abundant Dispenser colors

summary.title: Use Potions Before Your Pick
bullets: Drink potions and use Little Help first
Save regular pick for a primed Dispenser

summary.title: Manage Your Pool Wisely
bullets: Keep only marbles matching your current or next potions
Do not hoard useless colors

summary.title: Little Help Is an Investment
bullets: Net positive when it completes a 5+ point potion
Think of it as strategic, not as a penalty

summary.title: Race Toward Skill Tokens
bullets: 4 VP each plus tempo advantage
Prioritize 3rd-of-a-kind and 5th-different-kind completions

summary.title: Watch Your Opponents
bullets: Track what colors they need
Deny key marbles when it also benefits you

summary.title: Embrace the Chaos
bullets: Dispenser is random; stay flexible
Adaptability beats rigid planning

walkthrough.title: The Setup Pick with Little Help
walkthrough.text: The most powerful single-turn technique is using your Little Help to remove a marble that creates the perfect chain reaction setup for your regular pick. Before your turn, identify a spot where removing one specific marble would leave two same-color groups ready to collide if a second marble were removed. Use Little Help to take the first, then use your regular pick to trigger the cascade. This planned one-two punch consistently generates 6-8 marble turns.

walkthrough.title: Potion Synergy Combos
walkthrough.text: Some potion powers synergize beautifully. Sands of Time lets you reactivate a used potion, effectively doubling any other power. Drinking Sands of Time on the same turn you drink another potion gives you that power twice. Pair it with Abyssal Draft for up to 8 marbles from potions alone before your regular pick even happens. When choosing new potions, think about how their powers combine with ones you already have.

walkthrough.title: Color Scarcity Awareness
walkthrough.text: There are exactly 20 marbles of each color. In a 4-player game, marbles get absorbed into players' Pools and potions-in-progress, making certain colors temporarily scarce in the Dispenser. Track which colors are being heavily used and avoid drafting potions that need scarce colors. Conversely, if a color is flooding the Dispenser, grab potions that use it heavily. Swimming with the current is easier than against it.

walkthrough.title: The Skill Token Race
walkthrough.text: In competitive play, controlling the pace of the game through Skill tokens is critical. If you are ahead on points, aggressively completing potions to deplete the countdown stack forces opponents into a scramble. If you are behind, you want to slow down and deny Skill tokens by diversifying your potion types so you do not accidentally trigger the endgame. The countdown stack is a clock you can manipulate.

walkthrough.title: Defensive Marble Taking
walkthrough.text: When you spot a massive chain reaction setup on the Dispenser, consider whether your opponent (who goes before you refresh it) might claim it first. Sometimes taking a marble that disrupts an opponent's obvious chain is worth more than your own optimal pick. This is especially potent in 2-player games where you directly control half the Dispenser interactions. A denied 6-marble chain is a 6-marble swing.

walkthrough.title: Elixir of Blind Love as Disruption
walkthrough.text: The Elixir of Blind Love lets you steal all marbles from one opponent's Pool. In a strategic context, this is both offensive and defensive: you gain up to 3 useful marbles while emptying their reserves. Time this potion for when an opponent has a full Pool of colors matching their nearly-complete potion. The disruption can delay their completion by a full turn or more while accelerating yours.

walkthrough.title: Potion of Prismatic Joy Timing
walkthrough.text: The Potion of Prismatic Joy lets you place Pool marbles on any holes regardless of color. This is most powerful when your Pool is full of off-color marbles and you have a potion that just needs 2-3 more ingredients. Save this potion for exactly that moment. A well-timed Prismatic Joy can complete a potion instantly using marbles that would otherwise be worthless, turning garbage into gold.

walkthrough.title: Optimizing Potion Draft Order
walkthrough.text: When drawing new potions, do not just look at point values. Consider: Does this potion's color recipe match what is abundant in the Dispenser right now? Does completing it push me toward a Skill token? Does its power synergize with potions I already have? A 3-point potion that you can complete in 2 turns and whose power enables a big chain reaction is often better than a 5-point potion that will take 5 turns to finish.

walkthrough.title: The Bottom Row Gambit
walkthrough.text: Marbles at the bottom of each track are stable; they will not move unless directly taken. This means you can read the bottom row of the Dispenser as a known quantity for future planning. The Abyssal Draft potion takes from the bottom row specifically. If you see a bottom row loaded with colors you need, hold your Abyssal Draft for that moment. Other players may not even notice the bottom row as a resource since most attention goes to explosive mid-track picks.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Point Maximization
walkthrough.text: In the final 2-3 turns, every point matters. Calculate exactly: is taking a Little Help to complete one more potion net positive? (Almost always yes if the potion is worth 3+ points.) Should you drink a potion for its power or is it more valuable to just keep the points and not risk wasting the turn? Use your remaining potion powers aggressively. There is no reason to end the game with undrunk potions whose powers could have helped you score more.

summary.title: The Setup Pick with Little Help
bullets: Use Little Help to remove a marble that primes a chain
Regular pick then triggers the cascade: 6-8 marbles possible

summary.title: Potion Synergy Combos
bullets: Sands of Time doubles any other potion power
Combine powers for massive multi-marble turns

summary.title: Color Scarcity Awareness
bullets: 20 marbles per color; track which are tied up in Pools
Draft potions that use abundant colors

summary.title: The Skill Token Race
bullets: Ahead? Push countdown to force endgame
Behind? Slow down and avoid triggering it

summary.title: Defensive Marble Taking
bullets: Disrupt opponent chain setups when possible
A denied 6-marble chain is a 6-marble swing

summary.title: Elixir of Blind Love as Disruption
bullets: Steal opponent Pool when it is full and useful
Delays their completion while helping yours

summary.title: Potion of Prismatic Joy Timing
bullets: Place Pool marbles on any holes regardless of color
Save for when Pool is full of off-color marbles you can use

summary.title: Optimizing Potion Draft Order
bullets: Match potion recipes to Dispenser color abundance
Fast 3-point potions with good powers often beat slow 5-point ones

summary.title: The Bottom Row Gambit
bullets: Bottom row marbles are stable and predictable
Abyssal Draft targets them; plan around bottom row availability

summary.title: Endgame Point Maximization
bullets: Little Help is almost always net positive if it completes a 3+ point potion
Drink all remaining potions; undrunk powers are wasted opportunity

entries.definition: The plastic contraption at the center of the table with 5 slide tracks and a tank on top. Ingredient marbles roll down the tracks, and you pick from them each turn.
One of the 5 channels on the Dispenser where marbles line up in a column. Each track can hold up to 9 marbles, though the 9th marble at the top cannot be picked.
The reservoir at the top of the Dispenser where reserve marbles sit before rolling down into the slide tracks.
Each player's personal board with two Bunsen burners (Brewing area) on top and a small retort flask (Ingredient Pool) for storing up to 3 leftover marbles.
One of the two slots on your Desk where potion tiles are placed face-up (Recipe side) while being brewed. You always have 2 potions in progress.
The small retort flask on your Desk that holds up to 3 ingredient marbles between turns. You can freely move marbles between your Pool and your hand during your turn.
The physical marbles representing magical ingredients. 80 total, 20 of each of the 4 colors. You collect these from the Dispenser and place them on potion tiles.
One of the 4 ingredient types (typically light blue/white marbles). The purest fluid known in the world of magic, collected from perpetually depressed unicorns.
One of the 4 ingredient types (typically red marbles). Toxic fumes from angry dragons, prized for high magical reactivity. Never inhale them!
One of the 4 ingredient types (typically yellow marbles). An extremely viscous substance used to thicken volatile compounds. Collected from sneezing swamp ogres.
One of the 4 ingredient types (typically black marbles). A very powerful ingredient obtained by gently brushing a fairy's hair. Avoid happy thoughts!
A double-sided tile representing a potion recipe. The Recipe side (front) has colored holes to fill with marbles. The Completed side (back) shows the point value and power.
The front of a potion tile showing colored holes where ingredient marbles must be placed. A grey ribbon marks the recipe. This side faces up while brewing.
The back of a potion tile revealed when the potion is finished. Shows the point value, power icon, and potion name.
When two or more marbles of the same color collide after a marble is removed from a track, they explode and you collect all connected marbles of that color.
When an Explosion causes marbles to slide and trigger another Explosion of a different color, and potentially more after that. Chains can cascade multiple times.
The one mandatory marble you take from the Dispenser each turn. This is the only action that can trigger Explosions.
A token you take when asking for the Professor's help. Lets you take 1 extra marble (no Explosions triggered). Costs 2 points at end of game. Once per turn maximum.
Worth 4 points. Earned by completing 3 potions of the same kind or 5 different kinds. Each achievement can only be earned once per player.
A stack of Skill tokens (4/5/6 for 2/3/4 players) that triggers the end of the game when depleted. Additional Skill tokens come from the general pool.
The top section of your Desk with 2 Bunsen burners where your in-progress potion tiles sit. You always refill to 2 potions at end of turn.
The space below your Desk where completed potion tiles are placed. These potions can still be drunk for their powers.
A reminder token given to the starting player. It never changes hands and is used to determine when the final round ends (player to the right of First Player finishes last).
Power: Take 1 ingredient of your choice from the Dispenser (no Explosions). A flexible potion that lets you grab exactly the marble you need. Worth 5 points.
Power: Take 2 adjacent ingredients of different colors from the same slide track (no Explosions). Great for filling two color needs at once. Worth 4 points.
Power: Take up to 1 ingredient of each color from the bottom row of the Dispenser (max 1 per track, no Explosions). Can net you up to 4 marbles. Worth 4 points.
Power: Steal all ingredients from 1 opponent's Pool. A disruptive potion that robs an opponent of up to 3 stored marbles. Worth 4 points.
Power: Place all ingredients in your Pool on any holes of your incomplete potions, ignoring color requirements. Turns mismatched marbles into wildcards. Worth 3 points.
Power: Reactivate the effect of 1 potion you already used (drunk). Effectively doubles any potion power. Incredible synergy potential. Worth 3 points.
Power: Take 2 or more adjacent ingredients of the same color from one slide track (no Explosions). An advanced potion for grabbing large same-color groups. Worth 4 points.
Power: Discard up to 5 ingredients of 1 color from the same slide track back into the tank (no Explosions). Rearranges the Dispenser to your advantage. Worth 5 points.
Potion tiles marked with a special symbol that are drafted during setup. Each player begins the game with 2 starter potions on their Bunsen burners.
Activating a completed potion's one-time power by rotating it upside-down. Can be done anytime during your turn. The potion still scores its full point value.
When returning marbles (from completed potions or discarded extras), roll them randomly onto the lid. Do not aim for specific tracks. Move overflow marbles to the nearest track with space.
If players are tied on points, each tied player takes 1 marble from the Dispenser trying to trigger an Explosion. The player who collects the most marbles wins.
Potions are worth 3-5 points based on difficulty. Skill tokens are worth 4 points each. Little Help tokens cost -2 points each. Highest total wins.
